title: Shape of distributions
description: Practice describing the shapes of data distributions. Some distributions are symmetrical, perfectly balanced on the left and right. Other distributions are unbalanced. We say they are "skewed" and have "tails."

assessment_items.question_content:
Alejandro recorded how much sleep he got each night for a month.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these best describes the shape of the distribution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

###Which shape?

Notice how there's no tail on the right, but there's a long tail on the left. So the distribution is skewed to the left (or left-skewed).

###Answer

The distribution is skewed left.

options.alt: A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled Hours of sleep, from 0 to 12, in increments of 1. The number of dots above each number of hours of sleep is as follows. 1, 1; 3, 1; 4, 1; 5, 2; 6, 3; 7, 7; 8, 8; 9, 6.

assessment_items.question_content:
Here's a dot plot showing the daily high temperature of Edgeton, Iowa in July.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which of these best describes the shape of the distribution?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

###Which shape?

Notice how the left and right halves look like approximate mirror images. So the distribution is roughly (or approximately) symmetric.

###Answer

The distribution is roughly symmetric.

options.alt: A dot plot has a horizontal axis labeled, Temperature (degrees Celsius), from 18 to 26, in increments of 1. The number of dots above each temperature value is as follows. 19, 1; 20, 2; 21, 7; 22, 9; 23, 6; 24, 3; 25, 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
Here's a box plot that summarizes how many hot dogs contestants ate in a contest.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these best describes the shape of the distribution?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Which shape?

Notice how the data to the left of the median is much more spread out than the data to the right of the median. The right and left halves are *not* mirror images.

###Answer

The distribution is not symmetric.

options.alt: A horizontal box plot is labeled, Number of hot dogs, and marked from 20 to 36 in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 24 to 29. The box extends from 29 to 32 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 31. The right whisker extends from 32 to 34. All values estimated.

options.alt: A horizontal box plot is labeled, Number of pictures, and marked from 25 to 40, in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 25 to 29. The box extends from 29 to 35 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 32. The right whisker extends from 35 to 39. All values estimated.

assessment_items.question_content:
Here's a box plot that summarizes the number of lifeguards at each San Diego water park.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which of these best describes the shape of the distribution?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Which shape?

Notice how the data on the left and right of the median look like approximate mirror images. So the distribution is roughly (or approximately) symmetric.

###Answer

The distribution is approximately symmetric.

options.alt: A horizontal box plot is labeled, Number of lifeguards, and marked from 0 to 20 in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 1 to 4. The box extends from 4 to 12 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 8. The right whisker extends from 12 to 15. All values estimated.

assessment_items.question_content:
Here's a box plot that summarizes the number of goblins in each village.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which of these best describes the shape of the distribution?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Which shape?

Notice how the data to the right of the median is much more spread out than the data to the left of the median. The right and left halves are *not* mirror images.

###Answer

The distribution is not symmetric.

options.alt: A horizontal box plot is labeled, Number of goblins, and marked from 40 to 50 in increments of 1. A left whisker extends from 40 to 41. The box extends from 41 to 45 and is divided into 2 parts by a vertical line segment at 42. The right whisker extends from 45 to 50. All values estimated.

###How to describe shape

We say a distribution is **symmetric** if the right half of its graph is a mirror of the left half of its graph.

We say a distribution is **skewed** if one side of its graph is much longer than the other side. The direction of the skewness (left or right) is toward the longer tail.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###Which shape?

Notice how the data to the right of the median is much more spread out than the data to the left of the median. The right and left halves are *not* mirror images.

###Answer

The distribution is not symmetric.
